chunks.text: Charles Darwin, in 1831, set out on a voyage around the world on board H.
During the five years of that expedition
he visited many island along the coast of South America.
On the Galapagos, he continued to observe.
He noticed that each island held a species of finch with a slightly different beak.
Darwin collected many specimen and, after his return, spent long years in thought.
Why were there different animals on different islands?
Why had they adapted so perfectly to each environment?
In 1859, the publication of his book, On the Origin of Species, shook the world.
The concept of evolution shook the belief of the age.
Although there was fierce criticism, Darwin's theory, over time, came to be accepted.
Nature, quietly yet deeply, continues to change from generation to generation.
